# Trellis

> Trellis is a graph-native, code-first agentic state engine: an event-sourced causal graph with branching, decision traces, and realtime sync. It is the system of record for **decisions**, providing agents with durable, queryable, auditable memory.

Trellis is published as a single npm package (`trellis`) with subpath exports for each module. The kernel runs locally and is fully useful without a hosted server. The codebase is written in TypeScript for the Bun runtime.

## Core concepts

- **Op**: an immutable, content-addressed, optionally Ed25519-signed event. Ops form a causal stream (`.trellis/ops.json`) that is never rewritten.
- **Fact**: an entity-attribute-value triple derived from applying ops.
- **Entity**: a node in the graph identified by a stable string id (e.g. `BlogPost:abc`).
- **Milestone**: a human-curated narrative checkpoint spanning a range of ops. Replaces the `git commit` concept.
- **Garden**: auto-detected abandoned work surfaced for revival.
- **Decision trace**: a `vcs:decisionRecord` op capturing why an agent chose a particular tool call.

- [README.md](README.md): quick start, install, status
- [docs/THE-STORY.md](docs/THE-STORY.md): why Trellis exists, the agentic-state problem

- [docs/AGENTS.md](docs/AGENTS.md): how to build agents on Trellis
- [docs/CONTEXT-GRAPHS.md](docs/CONTEXT-GRAPHS.md): market thesis on agentic context
- [docs/VISION.md](docs/VISION.md): local-first agentic OS framing
- [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): current package layout and target runtime shape

## Subpath exports

Import each module from `trellis/<path>`:

- `trellis` — top-level engine, kernel, scaffold helpers
- `trellis/core` — TrellisKernel (generic graph CRUD, no VCS dependency)
- `trellis/vcs` — VCS engine, ops, types, semantic diff
- `trellis/cms` — content-collections client SDK, formula evaluator, consumer scaffolder
- `trellis/decisions` — decision trace recording, query, hook registry
- `trellis/ai` — embeddings, chunker, vector store
- `trellis/links` — wiki-link parsing and graph link resolution
- `trellis/server` — HTTP+WS server, auth, permissions, multi-tenancy, realtime, deploy
- `trellis/client` — typed client for the Trellis server
- `trellis/react` — provider + hooks for React consumers
- `trellis/db` — low-level EAV store APIs
- `trellis/persist/better-sqlite` — better-sqlite3 backend
- `trellis/persist/sqljs` — sql.js (browser) backend
- `trellis/persist/factory` — backend factory

## CLI

`trellis` is also a CLI. Common commands:

- `trellis init` — initialize a Trellis repo (creates `.trellis/`)
- `trellis status` — show branch, op count, tracked files
- `trellis log [--limit N]` — op history
- `trellis milestone create -m "msg"` — narrative checkpoint
- `trellis branch <name>` — create or switch branches
- `trellis issue create -t "title" -P high` — create a tracked issue
- `trellis garden list` — list abandoned work clusters
- `trellis sdiff <old> <new>` — AST-level semantic diff
- `trellis code` — start an agent harness session

Full reference: see the [CLI guide on trellis.computer](https://trellis.computer/docs/cli).

## Key differences from Git

- No staging area. Ops are created automatically when files change.
- Ops are immutable. No rebase, no `git reset --hard`.
- Three op tiers: Tier 0 (file), Tier 1 (structural), Tier 2 (semantic/AST).
- Milestones, not commits, are the narrative unit.
- The garden surfaces abandoned work; nothing is silently lost.

## Conventions when writing code with Trellis

- Use single-word variable names (`pid`, `cfg`, `err`, `opts`, `dir`).
- Prefer `const`. Use early returns instead of `else`.
- Database fields are snake_case (Drizzle convention).
- Never modify `.trellis/` directly; mutate via the kernel API.
- Use `trellis issue start TRL-N` to start tracked work — auto-creates a branch and assigns the agent.
